reviews.why: A is a profitability margin (mid-teens margin target); B is comparable/same-store sales growth (revenue). Different metric and mechanism, even though both concern the Flower Child brand.

Different scope, even though both touch COVID-era rules. "covid_restrictions" (side A) is the BROAD set of all government measures: "federal, state and local restrictions...to mitigate the spread of the COVID-19 virus" — covers closures, capacity limits, mandates, quarantines, etc. across the whole business. "in_person_dining_restrictions" (side B) is the NARROW, specific subtype: "restriction on in-person dining" — one particular operational limit (people can't eat inside the restaurant). Scope test fails: broad-bucket vs single-subtype is a parent-vs-child relationship, not the same object. Note side A's own 8-K quote even lists in-person-dining restriction as ONE EXAMPLE among COVID containment measures ("including the potential for...restriction on in-person dining"), which confirms B is a member-of A, not equal to A. Object also differs (all COVID restrictions vs dining-only restrictions) and mechanism differs (general operational/legal disruption vs specifically losing dine-in revenue). A child driver subsumed by a parent is a candidate for an "optional_link"/hierarchy, NOT a reversible SAME_AS merge. Merging would over-collapse a specific, separately-trackable demand driver into a catch-all bucket. Keep separate.

A is membership enrollment/acquisition (growing the member base); B is personalized/unpublished offers within the program (a marketing tactic to drive incrementality). Same program, but different object and mechanism.

A is landlords' demand to host CAKE concepts (high tenant demand, a growth tailwind); B is landlord readiness to deliver premises causing opening delays. Opposite mechanism and direction; 'landlord' is the only overlap.
A is the labor market (employment availability/cost affecting margins); B is entry into new geographic markets (expansion). 'market' is a homonym; unrelated objects and mechanisms.

A is borrowing availability under the revolver credit facility (financing); B is availability/selection of high-quality real-estate sites (expansion). 'availability' is a homonym; different object and mechanism.
A is the guest rewards/loyalty program (launch and rollout); B is lapsed guests' stated intent to return (traffic recovery). Different object and mechanism; only 'guest' overlaps.
A is efficiency of labor (workforce productivity driving margin); B is supply-chain/purchasing integration leveraging scale. Different object and mechanism; only 'efficiency' overlaps.

A is the overall average check per customer (driven by menu pricing plus mix); B is specifically check mix, the menu-item composition component. Mix is one input to average check, not the same metric; different scope.
A is off-premise sales mix (channel composition); B is the Flower Child rewards/loyalty program (app-based points). Same brand, but different object and mechanism.

Different objects: guest_satisfaction measures how happy current guests are (NPS/CSAT scores); lapsed_guest_traffic tracks customers who stopped visiting and their intent to return. Different metric and mechanism.

Same object: government-imposed COVID/pandemic containment restrictions affecting CAKE's restaurant operations. The two sides' quotes are near-identical in substance — side A's 2023-05-10 8-K quote ("the COVID-19 pandemic and related containment measures, including the potential for quarantines or restriction on in-person dining") and side B's 2024-02-21 8-K quote ("pandemics and related containment measures, including the potential for quarantines or restriction on in-person dining") are essentially the same sentence, with side B simply generalizing "COVID-19 pandemic" to "pandemics." Same scope: both describe restrictions on in-person dining at the same company (CAKE). Same mechanism: regulatory containment measures (quarantines, dining restrictions) that disrupt the restaurant business. The "covid" vs "pandemic" wording is a labeling difference for the identical risk, and side A's own variant list already includes "covid_dining_restrictions," confirming the dining-restriction framing belongs to the same object. All three tests pass — reversible SAME_AS warranted.
cash_on_cash_returns is a new-unit investment return target (20-25%); operating_cash_flow is company-wide cash generated from operations. Different metric, scope, and mechanism.

insurance_costs is general insurance cost pressure (unscoped, alongside wages); medical_insurance_costs is specifically employee health/medical insurance expense within labor. Different scope and mechanism — general insurance is broader than medical only.

All three tests pass. (1) Same metric: both track CAKE's forward guidance for the count of new restaurant openings in a fiscal year ("expect to open as many as N new restaurants in fiscal 20XX across our portfolio of concepts"). The quotes are near-identical in structure across both sides. (2) Same scope: both span the entire company portfolio of concepts (The Cheesecake Factory, North Italia, Flower Child, FRC) — not a single brand or segment. The "unit" in unit_development_guidance is just CAKE's term for a restaurant unit; the evidence quotes are literally about restaurant openings. (3) Same mechanism: both are management's prospective guidance on planned unit/restaurant development (new openings) issued in 8-K/10-Q/10-K filings. Side A even shares the exact fiscal-2026 "open as many as 26 new restaurants" language with side B. "restaurant" and "unit" are synonyms here for the same development-guidance object. Merge is reversible via SAME_AS.
AUV is sales/revenue per North Italia location; restaurant_level_margin is the profit margin (profitability). Same brand scope but different metrics — revenue vs margin.
new_restaurant_unit_volumes is sales per new unit (revenue); new_unit_returns is the investment return achieved on new units (profitability/return on capital). Different metric and mechanism.

off_premise_capacity is the ability/throughput to serve off-premise orders; off_premise_promotions is marketing offers driving off-premise sales. Different objects and mechanisms, only shared channel context.

consumer_spending is the broad consumer confidence/spending environment (all consumers); high_income_consumer_spending is specifically the top-end/high-income cohort and trade-down dynamics. Different scope — subset vs whole.
guest_rewards_program is a loyalty/marketing program (operational initiative with launch costs); guest_value_scores is guests' perceived value/price survey metric. Different objects and mechanisms.

Different mechanism: a tracks supply-chain cost savings ('supply chain savings will continue'); b tracks scale-leverage benefits ('leverage our scalable supply chain', 'benefit on increasing scale'). Cost reduction vs scale leverage are distinct mechanisms.

Same object (operating margin) but different scope: a is actual reported margin/income from operations; b is forward-looking guidance ('we anticipate building', 'wanted to get them back to 5.2%'). Actual vs forecast are different.

Same brand but different object: a is unit development/growth risk ('develop, grow and operate North Italia'); b is menu price increases ('another price increase in October for North Italia'). Expansion vs pricing.
Same metric type but different scope: a is Flower Child brand-specific off-premise mix (55%); b is company/Cheesecake Factory off-premise sales (~21-22%). Single brand vs whole-company scope differ.
Same brand but different object/metric: a is annual average unit volume ($4.4M AUV); b is average weekly sales ($85,200/week). Annualized per-unit volume vs weekly sales are distinct measures.
Different metric: a is industry sales (Black Box casual dining sales index/trends); b is industry traffic (BlackBox/NAPTRAC trends, traffic). Sales dollars vs guest traffic are different objects.

Different metrics: 'off_premise_mix' is the % of Flower Child sales coming from takeout/delivery (55% off-premise mix), while 'unit_count' is the number of physical locations (38 locations, target 700). Different object.

Different object: a is guest value-perception scores ('value scores are pretty stable'); b is lapsed-guest return intent/traffic ('60% of our lapsed guests have stated interest in coming'). Value perception vs lapsed-customer recovery.
Same brand but different metric: a is average check (dollar spend per customer, $34.60 lunch/$44.40 dinner); b is menu mix shift (-2%). Check size vs mix component differ.

Same base object (EPS) but different scope: a is reported diluted EPS level ($0.37, $0.68, etc.); b is EPS vs expectations/surprise ('surpassing expectations', 'exceeding our guidance'). Level vs surprise-vs-consensus differ.

All three tests pass for CAKE. (1) Same metric: both measure "net availability for borrowings" on the company's revolving credit line. Side A quotes "net availability for borrowings of $236.5M / $366.5M ... under the Revolver Facility"; Side B quotes "$366.5 million of availability on its revolving credit facility." The $366.5M figure even matches across both sides (B's 8-K dated 2025-04-30 reports the same number A later confirms in its 2025-11-03 10-Q). (2) Same scope: both are CAKE-only, same single Revolver Facility, same borrowing-capacity-remaining concept. (3) Same mechanism: availability = total facility minus outstanding debt minus standby letters of credit — i.e. undrawn capacity on the revolver. Side A's "credit facility" is the same instrument Side B names "revolving credit facility" (CAKE has one Revolver Facility). The names differ only in generic-vs-specific wording for the identical object. SAME_AS is reversible and safe to add.

Benefit vs cost. 'contribution' is the ~100bps sales/margin uplift the loyalty program delivers; 'costs' is the launch/marketing expense of the Rewards program (0.2%). Opposite sides of the same program = different metrics.

Different events on the instrument. 'issuance' is the act/amount of issuing convertible notes ($575M / $345M raised); 'maturity' is the due date (e.g. June 15 2026, March 15 2030). Issuing vs coming-due = different mechanism.

Cause vs outcome. 'construction_delays' is the outcome of delayed store openings (construction challenges, permit delays); 'labor_availability' is one input — availability of qualified tradespeople. Related but distinct objects.

Different scope and direction. 'dining_capacity_restrictions' is government-mandated limits on on-premise dine-in capacity; 'off_premise_capacity' is the expansion of off-premise (takeout/delivery) channel capacity. On-premise restriction vs off-premise expansion.

Level vs surprise. 'adjusted_eps' is the reported adjusted EPS figure ($0.39, $0.56, etc.); 'eps_surprise' is EPS performance relative to expectations/guidance (beat/miss, 'above the high end'). Absolute value vs deviation-from-expectation = different metrics.

General vs specific subset. 'insurance_costs' is broad insurance cost pressure (wage rates and insurance costs); 'self_insurance_cost' is specifically the company's retained-risk self-insurance costs driven by insurance-market conditions. Self-insurance is a narrower, distinct mechanism.

Different metrics. 'cash_capital_expenditure_guidance' is forecast capex spending in dollars ($165-175M, $190-210M); 'cash_on_cash_returns' is the % return on invested capital for new units (20-25% target). Spend vs return = different object.

Entirely different metrics sharing only the token 'net'. 'net_income_margin_surprise' is profit margin vs guidance (financial); 'net_promoter_score' is a customer-satisfaction/loyalty metric (NPS). No shared object, scope, or mechanism.

Total vs comparable. 'flower_child_sales' is absolute Flower Child revenue in dollars ($127.5M, $34.5M, includes new-unit growth); 'flower_child_same_store_sales' is comparable/same-store sales growth % excluding new units (comps up ~5-11%). Total revenue vs comp-sales metric differ in object and mechanism.

A is the risk of failing to develop/grow/operate the North Italia concept (expansion and execution). B is the North Italia comparable-sales metric (a sales-growth percentage). Different object: development/operations risk vs a sales metric. Same brand, but not the same thing.

A is government environmental rules (water usage, emissions, sanitation) imposing compliance burden. B is activism and litigation by advocates and opponents on environmental/social matters. Different object and mechanism: regulatory compliance vs public/legal activism pressure.

Both names refer to the same underlying driver — the level of consumer spending that funds restaurant demand — measured for the same company (CAKE), at the same scope (macro consumer demand feeding restaurant traffic/check), via the same mechanism (weaker economy/consumer confidence → less spending → fewer visits and lower checks → adverse results). Side A names the discretionary slice explicitly; side B's quotes ("consumer confidence and spending," "easing of the consumer," "trade down") are the same construct in broader words, and CAKE's own filings tie both to identical outcomes. All three SAME tests pass for this single-company pair.

A is the forward-looking revenue outlook the company provides (forecast). B is actual reported revenue versus expectations (beat/miss/in-line outcome). Different object and mechanism: a forecast issued vs an actual-vs-expected result.

A is targeted messaging strategy to drive specific dayparts (marketing tactic/effectiveness). B is marketing expense as a cost line item affecting operating margin. Different object: marketing strategy vs marketing cost.

Same metric type (comparable sales) but different scope. A is international/global comp sales across foreign geographies; B is domestic Cheesecake Factory comparable restaurant sales. Scope differs (international vs domestic namesake brand).

A is competition with other chains and independents for customer traffic (competitive dynamics). B is industry-wide casual dining sales trends (Black Box casual dining index). Different object: competitive pressure vs the industry sales level itself.

A is capturing market share by outperforming the casual dining industry in comps/traffic. B is entering new geographic markets (expansion/footprint growth). Different object and mechanism: share gains vs geographic expansion.
Same brand but different metric. A is Flower Child margin/profitability (mid-teens margin target). B is Flower Child sales/revenue dollars. Margin vs sales are different metrics.

A is closure of/damage to bakery manufacturing facilities, disrupting product supply to restaurants and customers. B is closure of restaurant locations affecting the unit portfolio. Different object and mechanism: supply-chain disruption vs store-count reduction.
Same Flower Child brand but different objects: average unit volume is a per-restaurant sales metric, while the rewards program is a points/loyalty app. Different object and mechanism.
Consolidated revenue is total company-wide revenue; international royalty revenue is a narrow royalty stream from international licensees. Different scope (whole vs subset) and object.
Commodity cost is the input expense level; commodity hedging is the financial-instrument practice of managing price risk. Different object and mechanism.
Capex is total capital expenditure; new_restaurant_capex is the subset spent specifically on new restaurant locations. Different scope (total vs subset).
New unit returns is the ROI/profitability of new units; new_unit_sales_transfer is cannibalization (sales pulled from existing nearby restaurants). Different metric and mechanism.
Minimum wage is the statutory wage-rate cost driver; wage_hour_litigation is lawsuits over wage/hour labor-law compliance. Different object and mechanism.

cash_on_cash_returns is a specific named profitability metric (annual cash flow / cash invested, 20-25% target); new_unit_returns is the broader general concept of returns on opening new locations. Both relate to new-store economics for CAKE but differ in specificity of object — one is a named ratio, the other a general return concept. Same-object test not clearly met.
Discretionary income is the money consumers have available; discretionary spending is the behavior of how they spend. Related macro drivers but distinct objects.
Breakage is unredeemed gift-card value recognized as revenue based on non-use; redemptions are cards actually being used. Opposite, distinct accounting objects.
Sales-to-investment ratio is a capital-efficiency metric; to-go packaging costs is an operating cost line item. Overlap is only the spurious token 'to'. Different object.
Same North Italia brand but different metrics: menu pricing actions vs the count of restaurant locations. Different object.

Opposite roles. third_party_bakery_sales is a REVENUE stream (CAKE bakery sells products TO third-party customers). third_party_vendor_failure is a supply RISK (vendors CAKE relies ON fail to deliver). Different object and mechanism; only shared tokens 'third party'.

Different financial instruments. convertible_notes_issuance is DEBT ($575M convertible senior notes). share_issuance is EQUITY (common stock issuance and dilution risk). Different object and mechanism; only shared token 'issuance'.

Opposite mechanisms. supply_chain_disruption is the NEGATIVE risk (shortages, delays, COVID/geopolitical harm). supply_chain_scale is the POSITIVE benefit (leveraging scalable supply chain, benefiting from increasing company scale). Same general domain but opposite direction/mechanism.

Different objects. north_italia_average_check is a per-customer spend METRIC ($34.60 lunch / $44.40 dinner). north_italia_development is the RISK of failing to develop/grow/operate the concept. Same brand, different metric vs risk; only shared tokens 'north italia'.

Different objects. lease_impairment is an EXPENSE/writedown on underperforming (urban, post-COVID) locations. operating_lease_liabilities is a BALANCE-SHEET obligation amount ($845.1M extension options). Charge vs liability; only shared token 'lease'.

Different objects and mechanisms. acquisition_contingent_consideration is the fair-value of deferred/contingent payment LIABILITIES (North Italia/FRC earn-outs). acquisition_integration is the operational RISK of combining companies (cultures, systems, employees). Financial liability vs integration risk; only shared token 'acquisition'.

restaurant_lease_renewal is a specific cost-RISK of being unable to renew leases on similar terms or relocate. restaurant_lease_terms is a general observation about the LEASING ENVIRONMENT terms being similar to historical expectations. Related lease concepts but one is a renewal cost-risk and the other a general market-terms commentary — same object/mechanism not clearly established.

Different scope. adverse_weather is the broad RISK-FACTOR bundle (weather conditions, natural disasters, public health emergencies affecting traffic/staffing) from 10-K risk factors. winter_weather is a specific single-quarter traffic impact (January cold) from a transcript. Broad risk factor vs one specific event; scope differs.

Different objects and mechanisms. gift_card_inventory_writedown is a one-time $7.9M write-down of physical gift card INVENTORY (an expense). gift_card_redemptions is customers REDEEMING gift card balances (revenue/liability movement). Asset writedown vs redemption activity; only shared tokens 'gift card'.

Same company (CAKE) and same underlying topic (opening new restaurant units), so mechanism overlaps. But the two sides are not cleanly the same object/scope. Side A (new_unit_development) is a BROAD activity/driver: it mixes the development program itself — actual openings ("we opened eight restaurants in Q1"), CapEx ("$25M... for new unit development"), revenue contribution ("revenue growth primarily driven by new restaurant openings"), AND forward plans. Side B (unit_development_guidance) is a NARROW forecast metric: all 3 quotes are purely the projected count the company expects to open ("expect to open as many as 25/26 new restaurants in fiscal 2025/2026"). So: Test 1 (same object/metric) is questionable — A = the activity/revenue driver, B = the guidance figure (a projection number), which is a different object. Test 2 (same scope) fails — A is a strict superset that even contains its own forward-guidance quotes, while B is a guidance-only slice. Test 3 (mechanism) passes. Because A's own evidence overlaps B (A engulfs B), it could be a sub-concept, but merging would collapse a forecast-metric node into a broad activity node and lose the activity-vs-forecast distinction. Object and scope differ / are unclear, so not a confident SAME; keep separate. UNCLEAR rather than a hard DIFFERENT because the topics genuinely overlap and B reads like a narrow guidance slice of A — a human reviewer should decide.

Opposite mechanisms. Disruption = shortages/delays as a headwind hurting openings and costs. Efficiency = integrating Flower Child purchasing into the broader supply chain to leverage scale (an improvement). Different object and direction.

Both drivers describe the same object/metric (immigration levels and immigration law/policy), the same scope (CAKE's ability to staff and pay restaurant labor), and the same mechanism (tighter immigration / reduced legal immigration shrinks the labor pool, raising labor costs and making hiring/staffing harder). The "immigration_policy" side even contains the exact "reduced levels of legal immigration ... significantly increase our labor costs and make it more difficult to fully staff our restaurants" quote that defines the "legal_immigration" driver, plus matching 10-K "immigration-related employment regulations may make it more difficult ... to identify and hire qualified staff" language. "legal_immigration" is a strict subset of the broader immigration-policy labor risk, with identical mechanism and scope. All three SAME tests pass; merge with a reversible SAME_AS.

Different object and scope. Guidance = forward total-company anticipated cash capex ('anticipate ~$165-175M'). restaurant_capital_expenditures = historical actual capex for the new-restaurant line item ('Capital expenditures for new restaurants...were $37.8M'). Forecast-total vs actual-line-item.

Same metric but different object. Guidance = expected forward margin ('we expect fiscal 2023 net income margin of ~4%'). Surprise = realized margin vs guidance ('adjusted net income margin of 5.9% well exceeded the high end of the guidance'). Forecast vs beat/miss outcome.

Different metric. Food cost = a single cost line ('opportunity to lower food costs'). Segment margin = overall segment profitability ('North Italia segment margins...mature units'). Cost component vs aggregate margin.
Different sales components. Alcohol mix = beverage category share/trade-down. Delivery mix = off-premise delivery share (~10% of sales). Distinct objects with no overlap.

Different object. Authorization = the board-approved program and remaining share limit ('authorized a share repurchase program of up to 61.0 million shares'). share_repurchase = actual buyback execution ('repurchased ~341,500 shares at a cost of $12.4 million'). Program framework vs executed activity.

Different metrics. Average weekly sales = absolute dollars per unit per week ('$85,200'). Same-store/comparable sales = percent growth at established units ('comparable sales increased approximately 6%'). Absolute AWS vs comp-sales growth rate.

Different metric; values literally differ in the same filing. Adjusted EPS excludes items ('adjusted net income per share...$0.39'); diluted GAAP EPS ('diluted net income per share...$0.37'). Non-GAAP adjusted vs GAAP diluted.

landlord_demand = real-estate developers/landlords wanting CAKE concepts as tenants in their projects. restaurant_industry_demand = broad consumer/industry demand environment. Different object and different scope; only 'demand' token overlaps.

intangible_asset_impairment = impairment of indefinite-lived intangible assets (trade names/goodwill). lease_impairment = impairment of lease/right-of-use assets at underperforming urban locations. Different asset objects; only 'impairment' overlaps.

company_owned_restaurant_count = total company-owned restaurants across all concepts. fox_restaurant_concepts_unit_count = unit count for just the FRC portfolio subset. Different scope (whole vs subset of the portfolio).

flower_child_acquisition = the corporate event of bringing Flower Child fully under CAKE's umbrella. flower_child_unit_count = count/growth metric of Flower Child locations. Different object (one-off acquisition event vs ongoing unit-count metric).

All three tests pass. Object/metric: both describe the risk that staff and others may unionize CAKE's workforce. The evidence quotes are literally the same boilerplate 10-K sentence ("Our staff members and others may attempt to unionize our workforce, establish boycotts or picket lines or interrupt our supply chains..."), just truncated in one side — same company (CAKE), same filing type (10-K), consecutive annual filings 2024/2025/2026. Scope: identical — the company's own employees/workforce, labor relations risk. Mechanism: identical — third parties (staff and others) attempting to unionize the workforce, with downstream boycotts/picket lines/supply-chain disruption limiting workforce management. "labor_unionization" and "workforce_unionization" are pure naming variants of the same driver backed by word-for-word identical evidence. Safe reversible SAME_AS.

Token overlap on "dining/restrictions" only — the underlying drivers differ on all three tests.  OBJECT/METRIC — different: - A (dining_capacity_restrictions): general government mandates a restaurant could face — capacity limits bundled with "shifts in consumer behavior, wage inflation, staffing challenges." The driver is broad regulatory/operating-cost pressure, not tied to any one trigger. - B (pandemic_dining_restrictions): specifically "pandemics and related containment measures, including quarantines or restriction on in-person dining." The driver is a pandemic-contingent demand shock.  SCOPE — different: - A names "capacity restrictions" as one example of ongoing government mandates affecting operations. No pandemic framing in the quote. - B is explicitly scoped to a pandemic scenario (quarantines, containment). It is conditional on a pandemic occurring, not a general operating condition.  MECHANISM — different: - A: regulation raises operating costs / limits seating capacity → margin/throughput pressure (sits alongside wage inflation and staffing). - B: a pandemic forces closures/containment → demand collapse for in-person dining (a discrete crisis event).  A general "capacity could be restricted by government" risk is NOT the same object as a "pandemic shuts down in-person dining" risk. One is a routine regulatory/cost driver; the other is a contingent crisis-demand driver. Different object, scope, and mechanism → keep separate. Same single company (CAKE) does not make them the same driver.

supply_chain_costs = supply-chain savings/cost outcome. supply_chain_efficiency = structural integration of Flower Child purchasing into the broader Cheesecake supply chain to leverage scale. Different object/mechanism (realized cost effect vs an operational integration action); only 'supply chain' overlaps.

environmental_social_activism = ESG activism/media campaigns/litigation risk. social_media_marketing = competitive social-media marketing/differentiation. Only the word 'social' overlaps; entirely different objects and mechanisms.

Same metric (CAKE four-wall / unit-level restaurant margin) and same scope, so tests 1 and 2 pass. Test 3 (mechanism) fails: 'four_wall_margin' captures the reported/actual metric (e.g., "improved to 15.4%", "consolidated four-wall margins of 15%... exceeding expectations"), while 'four_wall_margin_guidance' captures management's forward-looking target ("for next year is that we'd like to get 25 basis points of four-wall margin"). The catalog intentionally separates an actual-result driver from its guidance/forecast driver — the '_guidance' suffix is a deliberate, producer-agnostic distinction, not a synonym. An outcome and a management forecast behave differently as signals. The token-overlap suggester only fired on the shared words (four/margin/wall), which is precisely the base name plus the '_guidance' qualifier; that qualifier is the load-bearing meaning difference. Keep separate (no SAME_AS).

supply_chain_disruption = external supply shortages/transportation delays/COVID-tariff disruption (a risk causing harm). supply_chain_integration = CAKE's internal operational improvement integrating Flower Child into its supply chain. Opposite direction (external risk vs internal optimization), different mechanism; only 'supply chain' overlaps.

Different metric: egg_price is the commodity cost/inflation of eggs (food-cost pressure), egg_supply is the ability to source eggs (avian-flu shortage/availability). Cost vs availability are different objects and mechanisms.

Different scope and object: new_restaurant_margin = ramp-up of margins at newly opened units toward maturity; restaurant_margin_guidance = forward guidance on overall corporate restaurant margin (~15% target). New-unit ramp vs whole-portfolio guidance differ.

Different mechanism: acquisition = the M&A deal to bring Flower Child fully under the umbrella; integration = the post-deal operational folding-in process. Distinct events/objects despite the shared brand.

The word 'licensing' means different things: alcoholic_beverage_licensing = liquor-license regulatory compliance; international_licensing = franchise-style licensing agreements to open restaurants abroad. Different object and mechanism.
Same metric (comparable/same-store sales) and mechanism, but different scope: one tracks the Flower Child brand, the other the North Italia brand. Scope differs, so not the same.

Same metric type (restaurant count) but different scope: company_owned_restaurant_count is the total across all concepts; north_italia_restaurant_count is just the North Italia brand. Aggregate vs single-brand subset.
Different metric: off_premise_mix is the percentage of sales coming through off-premise channels; flower_child_sales is total dollar sales. Same brand but a ratio vs an absolute revenue figure.

Different scope: insurance_costs is broad/general insurance cost pressure (wage + insurance); workers_compensation_insurance_costs is the specific workers'-comp line tied to claim activity. The latter is a narrower subset, not the same object.

Revenue vs cost: alcoholic_beverage_sales is the share of revenue from alcohol; food_beverage_cogs is the cost of goods (food and beverage costs as % of sales). Opposite sides of the P&L, different objects and mechanisms.

Different channel/object: bar_sales_mix is the bar/alcohol share of sales; delivery_sales_mix is the delivery share of sales. Both are 'sales mix' but of distinct channels, so different scope and object.

Different scope. (a) is comparable sales for the Flower Child brand/concept specifically; (b) is same-store sales across international geographies (brand resonating worldwide). Different segment cut of comp sales, not the same metric on the same scope.

Different object. (a) is the COUNT/growth of FRC restaurant units (10-15% annual unit growth). (b) is unit VOLUMES, i.e., per-restaurant sales/revenue at new locations ('same unit volumes as the North system'). Count of stores vs sales-per-store are distinct metrics.

Different mechanism. (a) is supply chain COST savings/efficiency (a cost driver). (b) is supply chain DISRUPTION/shortages causing delays in opening restaurants (an operational risk/availability driver). Cost outcome vs availability/disruption are different drivers despite shared tokens.

Different object/metric. Four-wall margin is restaurant-level profitability (before corporate G&A); operating margin is company-wide operating profitability. These are distinct margin metrics at different organizational levels, so different scope and object.

Different object/scope. (a) is macro consumer discretionary spending and its effect on traffic/check (an external demand driver). (b) is the spending distribution across the company's own customer income cohorts (cohort mix stability). Macro spend vs internal customer-cohort spend pattern are different.

Completely different objects. (a) is availability under the revolving credit facility (financing/liquidity). (b) is tip credit wage rates affecting labor costs (wage/labor regulation). Share only the word 'credit' with unrelated meanings.

Different object. (a) is the existence/launch of the guest rewards (loyalty) program. (b) is the quantified sales CONTRIBUTION of the loyalty program (~100 bps). Program-as-initiative vs its measured financial contribution are distinct drivers; merging would conflate a program with its impact metric.

Different mechanism/scope. (a) is targeted daypart-specific marketing messaging using data to drive specific dayparts. (b) is creative marketing campaigns tied to events to generate publicity/engagement. Different marketing strategies/objectives, not the same driver.

Different scope. (a) is company-level (Cheesecake Factory flagship) average check; (b) is North Italia brand's average check specifically (lunch/dinner figures). Same metric type but different brand scope, so not the same driver.

Different mechanism. (a) is supply chain INTEGRATION improvements (integrating acquired brands' supply chains). (b) is leveraging supply chain SCALE/purchasing power as the company grows. Integration vs scale-leverage are distinct supply-chain levers despite shared tokens.

Both count restaurant units, but scope differs. A = Fox Restaurant Concepts (FRC) portfolio unit count (domestic owned concepts). B = number of international-licensed restaurants (foreign, licensed). Different segments/scopes, not the same metric.

Only the word 'market' overlaps. A = labor market conditions (wage/hiring environment affecting margins). B = CAKE taking competitive market share (sales/traffic vs peers). Different objects and mechanisms entirely.

A = financial leverage / debt-to-equity ratio (balance-sheet indebtedness). B = sales leverage = operating leverage of fixed costs against sales volume. Different object and mechanism; only token 'leverage' overlaps.
Same brand (North Italia) but different metric. A = company-owned restaurant count (unit count). B = comparable/same-store sales growth (sales metric). Different objects.

Same brand/menu, but A = menu mix (shift in product mix, the -2% mix effect) and B = menu pricing (price increases taken). Mix and pricing are distinct comp-sales components with different mechanisms.

Both are impairment charges but on different asset classes with different mechanisms. A = goodwill/indefinite-lived intangible impairment (annual qualitative/fair-value test). All three tests pass for CAKE (same company, same restaurant operating-cost line).  OBJECT/METRIC — same: Both track CAKE's "food and beverage costs" as a percent of revenue. Side A quote: "food and beverage costs were 23.4% for fiscal 2023 vs 24.6%... due to menu price increases in excess of inflation." Side B quote: "food and beverage costs were 23.5% and 25.2% in the third quarters of fiscal 2023 and 2022... due to menu price increases slightly in excess of inflation." These are the identical income-statement line item, expressed identically (percent of sales), with identical explanatory mechanism (menu price increases vs inflation). Side B's 8-K quotes ("Food and beverage costs 206,224 23.8%") are the same dollar+percent line. Side A's transcript quote explicitly equates this to COGS: "I realize COGS were a helper with that flat food cost" — confirming the "cogs" suffix and "costs" suffix point at the same thing.  SCOPE — same: Both are company-wide CAKE restaurant food-and-beverage cost ratio (consolidated, all categories), same single company (CAKE), overlapping fiscal periods (both cover fiscal 2023 Q3 etc.). No scope split (no segment/brand/region difference).  MECHANISM — same: Both attribute movement to "menu price increases in excess of inflation across most categories" — verbatim-matching driver mechanism.  The only difference is the name suffix: "_cogs" (cost of goods sold) vs "_costs". Side B already lists same_as_variants including cost_of_goods_sold, cost_of_sales, food_and_beverage_cost — i.e. these are recognized synonyms. The naming is a surface variant of one metric, not two metrics. Safe to add a reversible SAME_AS link.

Crux: the two drivers describe weather at different scopes and mechanisms, so they fail the SAME test.  Evidence read: - adverse_weather (CAKE 10-Ks): "Adverse weather conditions, natural disasters and public health emergencies can impact customer traffic, make it more difficult to fully staff our restaurants." This is the BROAD, ongoing-risk category — routine bad weather (rain, snow, cold) that lowers foot traffic and staffing, named alongside natural disasters and health emergencies as a general operating-condition risk. - severe_weather (CAKE transcript): "we're talking about unprecedented weather, the fires in Los Angeles, the holiday shifts." This is a SPECIFIC, acute, extreme/catastrophic event (LA fires, unprecedented conditions) tied to a particular period.  Three-part test: 1. Same object/metric? PARTIAL — both are "weather," but one is the general adverse-weather risk category, the other is an extreme/catastrophic event. Not clearly the same object. 2. Same scope? NO — adverse_weather = broad, recurring, everyday weather affecting traffic/staffing across the business; severe_weather = a specific extreme episode (LA fires, "unprecedented" weather). 3. Same mechanism? NO — adverse_weather works through reduced customer traffic + staffing difficulty as an ongoing risk; severe_weather is a discrete disaster-driven disruption. The token overlap ("weather") is surface-level only.  Note: adverse_weather's own variant list pairs it with "inclement_weather" (routine bad weather), reinforcing that adverse_weather = everyday weather risk, NOT catastrophic events. "Severe/unprecedented" weather (disasters) is a distinct, narrower concept.  At least two of the three conditions fail (scope and mechanism), and the first is unclear, so the verdict is DIFFERENT. Keep separate — do not add SAME_AS.

But "a" frames the object as the broad "licensing AND permitting process" run by government bodies, while "b" is specifically restaurant-build/opening permits. Not cleanly the same object.  2. SAME SCOPE — FALSE. This is the decisive break.    - a (government_permitting): the actor/cause is the GOVERNMENT itself being slow — "longer than usual GOVERNMENTAL delays in the licensing and permitting process." Its own variant is construction_permitting, but the named driver centers on government-side delay as the driver, not specifically restaurant openings.    - b (restaurant_permitting): scope is narrowly RESTAURANT OPENINGS — "Restaurant opening dates may be impacted by ... permit approval delays" / "opening timelines ... pressured by substantial permitting delays ... shifting some restaurant openings." The effect tracked is opening-schedule slippage for new units.  3. SAME MECHANISM — UNCLEAR/FALSE.     - a: mechanism = government bureaucracy is slower than usual (supply-side bottleneck at the regulator) affecting licensing+permitting generally.    - b: mechanism = permit approval lead times delaying new-restaurant openings (a unit-growth / development-pipeline mechanism), explicitly bundled with supply-chain challenges in two quotes.  The merge risk here mirrors the historical v2 over-merge failure: collapsing a broad government-process driver into a specific restaurant-opening-timeline driver because they share the word "permitting." They are related but distinct — government_permitting is about regulator slowness across licensing+permitting; restaurant_permitting is about new-unit opening delays from permit lead times. Scope differs (general licensing vs new-restaurant openings) and mechanism differs (regulator backlog vs development-pipeline lead time). Two of three tests fail, so SAME is not justified.  Keep separate (no SAME_AS). If anything, government_permitting could later be reviewed as a possible parent/contributing factor, but that is a links/optional relationship, not an identity merge.
