(b) is specifically lowering North Italia's food costs via Cheesecake best practices. Different scope (all new units vs. one brand) and different mechanism (ramp-up curve vs. best-practice deployment).

Different objects. (a) is the operational integration process of the Flower Child concept ('continue down the integration path'). (b) is the restaurant-level profit margin / margin target ('margin target ... mid-teens level'). Integration progress vs. profitability are distinct metrics with different mechanisms, even though both concern Flower Child.

Different metrics. (a) is average sales per restaurant / AUV ('annualized AUVs of $7.7 million'). (b) is the number of company-owned North Italia locations ('North Italia Company-Owned Restaurants'). Sales-per-unit vs. unit count are distinct objects with different mechanisms.

Only the word 'integration' overlaps, with opposite meaning. (a) is risk from combining acquired companies ('complexities associated with combining independent companies'). (b) is the cost/supply benefit of owning the bakery production ('vertical integration of the Cheesecake Factory bakeries'). Different objects, scope, and mechanism entirely.

Different metrics. (a) is the rate of operational incidents at on-premise dining ('on-premise incident rates remained above 2019 levels'). (b) is the share/level of dine-in traffic ('85% to 87% level of on-premise traffic'). Incident frequency vs. guest volume share are distinct objects with different mechanisms.

Different scope and object. (a) is the specific delivery-channel menu price premium ('about 2% to 3% versus the in-restaurant menu'). (b) is general ability to raise prices in a market ('sufficient pricing power in California'). Channel-specific premium vs. broad pricing capability are distinct.

Different objects. (a) is impairment of long-lived assets (the broad reported line 'impairment of assets', primarily long-lived asset write-downs). (b) is specifically lease impairment of urban locations ('predominantly lease impairment'). Lease impairment is a distinct component; asset impairment as defined centers on long-lived asset write-downs. Different object scope and mechanism, so keep separate.

Different metrics. (a) is the menu/sales mix shift at North Italia ('north Italia mix was negative 2%'). (b) is the segment profit margin ('North Italia segment margins'). Mix (sales composition) vs. margin (profitability) are distinct objects with different mechanisms, despite same brand.

A = benefit from owned vertical integration of the Cheesecake bakeries (in-house bakery production). B = general supply-chain integration improvements. A is a specific owned-asset advantage; B is a broader operational integration. Different object/scope.

A = demand for branded CPG/retail products (pressure like other CPG companies). B = demand across the restaurant dining industry. Different object and scope (packaged retail goods vs restaurant dining).

A = food-cost inefficiency at new restaurants ramping toward target margins (actual-to-theoretical gap). B = integrating Flower Child purchasing into the broader supply chain to leverage scale. Different object and mechanism.

Different metric: 'a' is the consolidated revenue level/growth; 'b' is revenue performance relative to the guidance range (beat/in-line/miss). Absolute revenue vs revenue-vs-expectations are distinct.
Different metric: 'a' is dollar sales driven by patio locations; 'b' is patio utilization (whether patios are used, impacted by hot weather). Sales dollars vs usage rate are distinct measures.

Different metric: 'a' is capacity/throughput expansion from the on- to off-premise shift; 'b' is off-premise sales volume and its share/mix of restaurant sales (~21-22%). Capacity vs sales/mix are distinct measures.

Different object: 'a' is developing/growing and operating the North Italia concept (expansion execution risk); 'b' is the North Italia traffic metric (customer count, e.g. -4%/-6%). Growth strategy vs a measured traffic figure.
Different metrics for the same brand: 'a' is menu/sales mix percentage; 'b' is the count of company-owned North Italia restaurants. Mix % vs unit count are distinct measures.

Different mechanism: 'a' is restaurant-level food efficiency/labor productivity; 'b' is supply-chain/purchasing integration leveraging scale. Operations efficiency vs procurement efficiency are different objects.

Different object/mechanism: 'a' is the risk of combining acquired independent companies (businesses, employees, cultures, systems); 'b' is integrating a brand's purchasing into the shared supply-chain operation. Distinct integration types.

a = the tip credit wage rate (a labor-cost / minimum-wage regulation item); b = wage-and-hour lawsuits (litigation risk). Different object and mechanism.

Both names point to the exact same CAKE line item, just labeled at different specificity. Object/metric: both are the company's "impairment of assets and lease termination" charge, which CAKE itself repeatedly says is "primarily related to the impairment of long-lived assets." The two sides even cite the SAME source documents and SAME dollar figures (e.g., 10-K 0001104659-24-027565 $29.5M and $31.4M appear on BOTH sides; 10-K 0001104659-26-018643 $23.0M appears on both; 10-K 0001410578-25-000195 $13.6M appears on both). Scope: same single company (CAKE), same restaurant-location property impairments. Mechanism: same accounting event — writing down long-lived store/location assets, reported together with lease termination. The "asset_impairment" side is the broad label and "long_lived_asset_impairment" is the precise label for the identical charge; the long_lived side's own evidence is verbatim the same sentences as the broad side. All three tests (same object, same scope, same mechanism) pass. Add reversible SAME_AS.

Different scope/granularity. severe_weather = extreme/severe events specifically (unprecedented weather, LA fires). weather = the general weather-impact concept (inclement weather, ordinary weather impacts on traffic, included in same_as with weather_conditions/weather_disruption). The general 'weather' bucket is broader and includes ordinary weather; severe_weather is a narrower extreme-event subset. Same/scope unclear and arguably different — keep separate. DIFFERENT.
